title: orophea aversa
intro: Orophea aversa, also known as Indian apple or Indian plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, and its medicinal properties include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent. The health benefits of Orophea aversa include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as providing relief from respiratory issues. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruits can be made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, while its roots are used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to create various concoctions.
